title: Emily Blunt Rejects AI in Steven Spielberg’s ‘Disclosure Day’ Scene, Favors Human Performance
description: Emily Blunt reveals why she refused AI for a pivotal scene in Steven Spielberg’s new sci-fi film Disclosure Day, opting for raw, human-made sound design instead. Discover her stance on artificial intelligence, Spielberg’s views on tech in filmmaking, and how practical recordings shaped the film’s eerie voice work.
content: Emily Blunt's AI remarks are drawing attention after the actor revealed she consciously avoided using artificial intelligence while filming a major sequence in Steven Spielberg’s upcoming sci-fi feature, Disclosure Day. Speaking about the experience,... [1814 chars]

title: Adani says US legal issues behind it, bets on AI-driven infrastructure boom
description: NEW DELHI, May 31:  Billionaire Gautam Adani said the Adani Group has moved beyond its US legal challenges and is accelerating investments across energy, transport, logistics and digital infrastructure, positioning itself to benefit from rising demand for artificial intelligence-led growth. In his annual letter to shareholders, Adani said the conglomerate remained committed to expansion despite heightened scrutiny over the past year, adding that matters related to the group’s US legal proceedings were now “behind us”, allowing it to focus on […]

title: India and US push deeper AI and chip ties as firms eye access to next-generation technologies
description: A new chapter in Indo-American relations is unfolding as India and the US deepen their partnership in artificial intelligence and semiconductor industries. This initiative encourages cross-border business collaboration, designed to bolster technological integration and attract crucial investments. Conversations have highlighted the importance of funding strategies, establishing robust AI infrastructure, and improving access to computing capabilities.

title: Editorial: Preserving humanity amid AI revolution
description: Artificial intelligence seems to have found its way into almost everything. Schools are trying to balance using it in some areas while weeding it out in others. Some universities are returning to handwritten blue book exams to ensure original thought. Others are using artificial intelligence to screen assignments for signs
